title: Peppery American Vegetable Casserole
description: A hearty, shaggy casserole featuring a mirepoix base and a blend of American spices, this dish is perfect for a festive gathering. It is also shojin-friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diets.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions, carrots, and celery to the skillet and cook until they are tender, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked mirepoix mixture, green beans, corn kernels, and golden caster sugar.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the whole wheat breadcrumbs evenly over the top of the casserole.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove the casserole from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
